tier1_description: High-stakes files requiring full TDD ceremony before production edits. (Service preset; copy from .tdd/presets/library.json or .tdd/presets/cli.json for other project types.)
_test_file_policy_doc: Phase-aware test-file policy. Replaces the older allow_test_file_edits_without_gate flag. Test edits are allowed during spec/red phases (writing failing tests), denied after red is confirmed (the documented 'don't edit tests in green phase' rule). Set allow_after_red_confirmed: true only when the operator explicitly authorizes return to red — document reason in commit message.
_markers_doc: Four-marker TDD ceremony. Edit-time hooks (require-tdd-state, require-second-opinion Tier-1 leg) require the first three; commit-time hook (gate-tier1-commit) additionally requires the fourth. Setting any marker without the matching operator APPROVED reply is self-approval and is forbidden.
_marker_aliases_doc: Backwards-compat aliases. The hook accepts the old name but emits a stderr deprecation warning. Drop in next major version (2026-Q3 target).
_legacy_required_markers_doc: Used by the existing require-tdd-state.sh path for backwards-compat. Reads the new edit-time markers; old name is honored via alias.
_integration_guards_doc: Project-level static-analysis guards. Each guard is a regex pattern that, if matched in a file outside allowed_globs, fails the green commit. Use to encode invariants that aren't easily expressed as integration tests (e.g., 'no direct calls to API X outside wrapper Y'). Guards are FALLBACK protection — write integration tests first; add a guard for invariants tests can't reach. See .claude/rules/go-integration-guards.md for the decision tree.

_integration_guards_schema.name: short identifier shown in violation message
_integration_guards_schema.pattern: POSIX extended regex (grep -E), matched against file content
_integration_guards_schema.severity: deny | warn — deny blocks the commit; warn only logs
_integration_guards_schema.allowed_globs: list of glob patterns; files matching any are exempt. Supports ** for cross-directory.
_integration_guards_schema.rationale: one sentence explaining what invariant this guards

_integration_guards_examples_doc: The empty array is the default. Each project should add its own guards over time — every shipped bug that a grep could have caught becomes a permanent guard.
